\"\"\"\n461\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n462\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n463\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n464\t    surface_id = get_intent_router(store)\n470\t\n471\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n472\t    if not utterance_id:\n473\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n474\t\n475\t    # Create utterance record\n476\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n477\t\n478\t    # Route the utterance\n479\t    try:\n480\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n481\t            utterance=utterance,\n482\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n483\t            session_id=session_id,\n484\t        )\n485\t\n486\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n487\t        intent_tasks = []\n488\t        intent_ids = []\n489\t\n490\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n491\t            # Create intent record in store\n492\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n493\t            await store.create_intent(\n494\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n495\t                session_id=session_id,\n496\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n497\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n498\t            )\n499\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n500\t\n501\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n502\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n503\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n504\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n505\t            )\n506\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n507\t\n508\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n509\t        async def stream_results():\n510\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n511\t            results = []\n512\t\n513\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n514\t                try:\n515\t                    result = await task\n516\t                    results.append(result)\n517\t\n518\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n519\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n520\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n521\t                            SSEEvent(\n522\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n523\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n524\t                                data={\n525\t                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n526\t                                    \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n527\t                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n528\t                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n529\t                                }\n530\t                            )\n531\t                        )\n532\t\n533\t                except Exception as e:\n534\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n535\t                    results.append({\n536\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n537\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n538\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n539\t                    })\n540\t\n541\t            return results\n542\t\n543\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n544\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n545\t\n546\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n547\t        return {\n548\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n549\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n550\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n551\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n552\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n553\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n554\t        }\n555\t\n556\t    except Exception as e:\n557\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n558\t        return JSONResponse(\n559\t            status_code=500,\n560\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n561\t        )\n562\t\n563\t\n564\t@app.post(\"/escalate\")\n565\tasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n566\t    \"\"\"\n567\t    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async _background_processor.stop()\n    if _context_warmer:\n        await _context_warmer.stop()\n    if _ambient_monitor:\n        await _ambient_monitor.stop()\n    if _broadcaster:\n        await lifespan=lifespan)\n\n\nasync def get_store():\n    \"\"\"Get or initialize the global session store.\"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        _store = {\"status\": \"ok\", \"service\": \"adc-voice\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/\")\nasync def serve_canvas():\n    \"\"\"\n    Serve the canvas HTML page (Phase 0 minimal surface).\n\n    This is the main entry     return\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"audio\")\n    surface_id = audio_surface_id\n    logger.info(f\"Registered audio surface: {surface_id}\")\n\n    # Load voice prompt\n    voice_prompt = load_voice_prompt(VOICE_PROMPT_PATH)\n\n    # Surface switch handler for audio-to-canvas continuity\n    async def on_surface_switch(surface_type: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle surface switch event (audio -> canvas).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Surface switch: {surface_type}\")\n        try:\n            result = await handle_surface_switch(surface_type, session_id, voice)\n            # Send acknowledgment to client\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n                \"surface\": surface_type,\n                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": completion\n    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n    if memory_handler:\n        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n        )\n\n        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n            description=(\n                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data={\n                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                                    \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                                }\n                            )\n                        )\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n This module provides:\n    - Audio file transcription\n    - Fallback for browsers without Web Speech API\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # whisper-stt service URL (Tailscale mesh)\n    # Configurable via ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL env var\n    DEFAULT_STT_URL = \"https://whisper.ardenone.com\"\n\n    def __init__(self, stt_url: str | None = None):\n        self.stt_url = stt_url or os.getenv(\"ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL\", self.DEFAULT_STT_URL)\n        self._is_available = None  # None = unknown, True = available, False = unavailable\n        self._failure_count = 0\n\n    async def transcribe(\n        self,\n        audio_data: bytes,\n        audio_format: str = \"webm\",\n    ) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Transcribe audio data to text using whisper-stt.\n\n        Args:\n            audio_data: Raw audio bytes (webm/opus format from MediaRecorder)\n            audio_format: Audio format hint (default: \"webm\")\n\n        Returns:\n            Transcribed text, or None if transcription failed.\n\n        Raises:\n            httpx.RequestError: If the request fails\n            httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the request returns an error status\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # whisper-stt uses /transcribe endpoint\n                response = await client.post(\n                    f\"{self.stt_url}/transcribe\",\n                    files=files,\n                    data=data,\n                )\n\n                if response.status_code == 200:\n                    result = response.json()\n                    text = result.get(\"text\", \"\").strip()\n\n                    if text:\n                        logger.info(f\"STT transcription successful: {text[:50]}...\")\n                        self._is_available = True\n                        return text\n                    else:\n                        logger.warning(\"STT returned empty transcription\")\n                        self._is_available = False\n                        return None\n                else:\n                    error_msg = f\"status {response.status_code} - {response.text}\"\n                    self._handle_transcription_failure(error_msg)\n                    return None\n\n        except httpx.RequestError as e:\n            error_msg = f\"request error: {e}\"\n            self._handle_transcription_failure(error_msg)\n            return None\n        except Exception as e:\n            error_msg = f\"unexpected error: {e}\"\n            self._handle_transcription_failure(error_msg)\n            return None\n\n    async def check_available(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if whisper-stt service is available.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n                # Try to reach the health endpoint\n                response = await client.get(\n                    f\"{self.stt_url}/health\",\n                )\n                is_available = response.status_code == 200\n                self._is_available = is_available\n                return is_available\n        except Exception:\n            self._is_available = False\n            return False\n\n    def get_status(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the current STT service status.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with keys:\n            - available: bool or None (None = unknown yet)\n            - stt_url: str\n            - failure_count: int\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"available\": self._is_available,\n            \"stt_url\": self.stt_url,\n            \"failure_count\": self._failure_count,\n        }\n\n    def _handle_transcription_failure(self, error_context: str = \"\"):\n        \"\"\"Handle a transcription failure.\"\"\"\n        self._is_available = False\n        self._failure_count += 1\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"STT transcription failed at {self.stt_url}. \"\n            f\"Error: {error_context if error_context else 'unknown error'}. \"\n            f\"Failure #{self._failure_count}.\"\n        )\n\n\n# Global STT fallback instance\n_stt_fallback: Optional[STTFallback] = None\n\n\ndef get_stt_fallback() -> STTFallback:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global STT fallback instance.\"\"\"\n    global _stt_fallback\n    if _stt_fallback is None:\n        _stt_fallback = STTFallback()\n    return _stt_fallback\n","numLines":151,"startLine":1,"totalLines":151}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c1ca2eb25c014a24b4ca6b7e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n4\t\n5\tCLI alias: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## How It Works\n10\t\n11\t1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n12\t\n13\t2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## Quick Start\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\t# Clone and install\n87\tgit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\n88\tcd aide-de-camp\n89\t\n90\tpip install -e \".[dev]\"\n91\t# or\n92\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n93\t\n94\t# Start the server\n95\tuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n96\t\n97\t# Verify it's running\n98\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\tOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n102\t\n103\tFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n104\t\n105\t---\n106\t\n107\t## CLI Usage\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\n111\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n112\t\n113\t# Query a specific topic\n114\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n115\t\n116\t# Current session summary\n117\tadc status\n118\t\n119\t# List active topic cards\n120\tadc topics\n121\t\n122\t# Show exception queue\n123\tadc exceptions\n124\t\n125\t# Configure the CLI\n126\tadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\n127\tadc config --set-session <session-id>\n128\t\n129\t# Help\n130\tadc --help\n131\t```\n132\t\n133\t---\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\t### Environment variables\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n140\t|----------|---------|---------|\n141\t| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n142\t| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n143\t| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n144\t| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |\n145\t| `ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL` | Whisper STT service URL for browser speech-to-text fallback | `https://whisper.ardenone.com` |\n146\t\n147\tThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n148\t\n149\t### Prompts directory\n150\t\n151\tAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n152\t\n153\t| File | Purpose |\n154\t|------|---------|\n155\t| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n156\t| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n157\t| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n158\t| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n159\t\n160\t### Project registry\n161\t\n162\t`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n163\t- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n164\t- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n165\t- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n166\t\n167\t---\n168\t\n169\t## Architecture\n170\t\n171\t### Key source files\n172\t\n173\t| File | Responsibility |\n174\t|------|---------------|\n175\t| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n176\t| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n177\t| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n178\t| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n179\t| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n180\t| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n181\t| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n182\t| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n183\t| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n184\t| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n185\t| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n186\t| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n187\t| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n188\t| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n189\t\n190\t### Runtime breakdown\n191\t\n192\t| Runtime | Used for |\n193\t|---------|---------|\n194\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n195\t| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n196\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n197\t| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n198\t\n199\t### Data flow\n200\t\n201\t```\n202\tPOST /dispatch\n203\t  → intent router (LLM classify)\n204\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n205\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n206\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n207\t  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n208\t  → cards rendered / updated in place\n209\t```\n210\t\n211\t---\n212\t\n213\t## Requirements\n214\t\n215\t- Python 3.11+\n216\t- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n217\t- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n218\t\n219\t---\n220\t\n221\t## Versioning\n222\t\n223\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |\n| `ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL` | Whisper STT service URL for browser speech-to-text fallback | `https://whisper.ardenone.com` |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n\n### Prompts directory\n\nAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n\n### Project registry\n\n`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Key source files\n\n| File | Responsibility |\n|------|---------------|\n| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n\n### Runtime breakdown\n\n| Runtime | Used for |\n|---------|---------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n\n### Data flow\n\n```\nPOST /dispatch\n  → intent router (LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n  → cards rendered / updated in place\n```\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Python 3.11+\n- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n\n---\n\n## Versioning\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. The 'Core Architecture' hot path also lists 'STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)'.\n\nReality: src/canvas/index.html uses only window.SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition (~line 699). In browsers without Web Speech API (Firefox, many WebViews) the mic button is simply disabled — there is no whisper fallback. The only whisper usage is OpenAI's hosted whisper-1 inside the Realtime voice session (src/realtime/session.py ~line 125), which requires OPENAI_API_KEY and is a separate path from canvas text dispatch.\n\nTasks:\n1. Add a server-side transcription endpoint (e.g. POST /api/v1/stt) that accepts recorded audio (webm/opus from MediaRecorder) and forwards it to the already-deployed whisper-stt service; make the whisper-stt URL configurable via env var with a documented default\n2.
